plan_section: Step 1: Add the upstream utilities submodule and submodule health checks

plan_alignment.notes: The staged changes implement the Step 1 scope: submodule metadata and gitlink, Node health check, package script, ESLint vendor ignore, recursive CI checkout, and quality-job submodule check. Docker runtime remains independent.

correctness_safety.notes: The health check validates presence, non-empty checkout, required upstream files, and a readable 40-character git SHA with actionable initialization guidance. CI now initializes submodules before jobs that may build or inspect the repo.

code_quality.notes: The script is straightforward, uses Node built-ins only, has clear failure paths, and keeps required upstream file contracts localized to the check script.

completeness.notes: Acceptance criteria are satisfied by the staged diff and reported verification: submodule pointer is pinned, check command passes, CI checkout is recursive, lint/typecheck pass, and no submodule contents are edited.

patterns.notes: The implementation follows the planned read-only submodule pattern and avoids adding runtime imports or production dependencies on upstream Flask/Jinja/static code.

integration.notes: Package scripts, CI steps, ESLint ignores, and Docker runtime behavior integrate cleanly with the existing project structure.

flags.note: Unstaged .forge changes are present in git status outside the provided staged review scope; ensure they are intentionally excluded from the implementation commit unless requested.
